message:
feat: update robot module and add golden test output fixtures

- src/robot.rs: continued robot module enhancements from concurrent work
- docs/FEATURE_PARITY.md: updated feature parity tracking
- tests/fixtures/golden/: new golden output fixtures for insanely_fast and
  whisper_cpp engines across code_switching, noisy_environment,
  short_utterance, and variable_volume_overlap test scenarios

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@ Features originating from or inspired by the whisper.cpp (C/C++) project.
 | Word-level timestamps | legacy_whispercpp | `backend::whisper_cpp` | Done | Parsed from whisper-cli JSON output into `TranscriptionSegment` with `start_sec`/`end_sec`. Split-on-word boundary mode (`-sow`) supported. |
 | Decoding parameters | legacy_whispercpp | `model::DecodingParams`, `backend::whisper_cpp` | Done | Full passthrough: best_of, beam_size, max_context, max_segment_length, temperature, temperature_increment, entropy_threshold, logprob_threshold, no_speech_threshold. |
 | Multiple output formats | legacy_whispercpp | `model::OutputFormat`, `backend::whisper_cpp` | Done | Supports txt, vtt, srt, csv, json, json-full, lrc. Artifact paths collected and returned in result. |
-| ffmpeg normalization | legacy_whispercpp | `audio` | Done | Converts any input to 16kHz mono PCM WAV via ffmpeg subprocess. Cancellable with timeout. Duration probing via ffprobe. |
+| ffmpeg normalization | legacy_whispercpp | `audio` | Done | Primary path converts input to 16kHz mono PCM WAV via ffmpeg subprocess (cancellable with timeout). When ffmpeg is unavailable, file-based normalization falls back to built-in Rust decode/resample for common formats. Duration probing remains ffprobe-based when available. |
 | Microphone capture | legacy_whispercpp | `audio`, `model::InputSource::Microphone` | Done | Captures via ffmpeg with platform-specific defaults (ALSA on Linux, AVFoundation on macOS). Configurable device, format, source, and duration. |
 | Confidence scoring | legacy_whispercpp | `model::TranscriptionSegment`, `accelerate` | Done | Per-segment confidence field parsed from backend output. Acceleration module normalizes confidence scores (softmax) with optional GPU path and deterministic CPU fallback. |
 | Quantized model support | legacy_whispercpp | `backend::whisper_cpp` | Done | Model path passthrough via `-m` flag. Quantized ggml models (Q4/Q5/Q8) usable by providing the model file path. |

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+{
+  "text": "We start in English before switching languages mid sentence. Ahora cambiamos a espanol para validar code switching estable. On bascule en francais puis on revient sans perdre le contexte. Wir wechseln kurz ins Deutsche und keep the same intent. Finally we return to English to close the mixed language sample.",
+  "chunks": [
+    {"text": " We start in English before switching languages mid sentence.", "timestamp": [0.0, 3.6]},
+    {"text": " Ahora cambiamos a espanol para validar code switching estable.", "timestamp": [3.6, 7.4]},
+    {"text": " On bascule en francais puis on revient sans perdre le contexte.", "timestamp": [7.4, 11.0]},
+    {"text": " Wir wechseln kurz ins Deutsche und keep the same intent.", "timestamp": [11.0, 14.8]},
+    {"text": " Finally we return to English to close the mixed language sample.", "timestamp": [14.8, 18.2]}
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+{
+  "text": "Background fan noise is present but speech remains intelligible. Street traffic rises while the speaker confirms checkpoint alpha. A keyboard clatter appears and the transcript should stay stable. The channel briefly clips, then recovers without dropping words. Final noisy segment confirms robustness under layered interference.",
+  "chunks": [
+    {"text": " Background fan noise is present but speech remains intelligible.", "timestamp": [0.0, 3.2]},
+    {"text": " Street traffic rises while the speaker confirms checkpoint alpha.", "timestamp": [3.2, 6.7]},
+    {"text": " A keyboard clatter appears and the transcript should stay stable.", "timestamp": [6.7, 9.9]},
+    {"text": " The channel briefly clips, then recovers without dropping words.", "timestamp": [9.9, 13.4]},
+    {"text": " Final noisy segment confirms robustness under layered interference.", "timestamp": [13.4, 16.8]}
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+{
+  "text": "Yes. No. Maybe. Go. Stop. Done.",
+  "chunks": [
+    {"text": " Yes.", "timestamp": [0.0, 0.6]},
+    {"text": " No.", "timestamp": [1.1, 1.6]},
+    {"text": " Maybe.", "timestamp": [2.2, 2.9]},
+    {"text": " Go.", "timestamp": [3.4, 3.9]},

+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+{
+  "text": "Speaker one starts softly and states the rollout status. Speaker two cuts in loudly with an urgent correction. Speaker one responds at medium volume and clarifies scope. Speaker three overlaps quietly to request a repeat. All speakers align on the final action and volume normalizes.",
+  "chunks": [
+    {"text": " Speaker one starts softly and states the rollout status.", "timestamp": [0.0, 2.2]},
+    {"text": " Speaker two cuts in loudly with an urgent correction.", "timestamp": [2.2, 4.5]},
+    {"text": " Speaker one responds at medium volume and clarifies scope.", "timestamp": [4.5, 6.7]},
+    {"text": " Speaker three overlaps quietly to request a repeat.", "timestamp": [6.7, 9.0]},
+    {"text": " All speakers align on the final action and volume normalizes.", "timestamp": [9.0, 11.4]}
+  ]
+}

+{
+  "text": "We start in English before switching languages mid sentence. Ahora cambiamos a espanol para validar code switching estable. On bascule en francais puis on revient sans perdre le contexte. Wir wechseln kurz ins Deutsche und keep the same intent. Finally we return to English to close the mixed language sample.",

+    {"start": 0.0, "end": 3.6, "text": " We start in English before switching languages mid sentence.", "avg_logprob": -0.24, "no_speech_prob": 0.01},
+    {"start": 3.6, "end": 7.4, "text": " Ahora cambiamos a espanol para validar code switching estable.", "avg_logprob": -0.23, "no_speech_prob": 0.01},

A keyboard clatter appears and the transcript should stay stable. The channel briefly clips, then recovers without dropping words. Final noisy segment confirms robustness under layered interference.",

+    {"start": 0.0, "end": 3.2, "text": " Background fan noise is present but speech remains intelligible.", "avg_logprob": -0.24, "no_speech_prob": 0.01},
+    {"start": 3.2, "end": 6.7, "text": " Street traffic rises while the speaker confirms checkpoint alpha.", "avg_logprob": -0.23, "no_speech_prob": 0.01},
